design_evolution_notes.description: The paper shows a proposed rectangular microstrip patch antenna in Fig. 2 and then identifies the simulated 'final patch obtained' in Fig. 3. Table I provides the finalized numerical values used for the canonical design.
design_evolution_notes.label: proposed-to-final refinement

design_evolution_notes.description: The final design is characterized by simulated return loss, VSWR, input impedance, radiation pattern, current distribution, and Smith chart results at 2.25 GHz.
design_evolution_notes.label: simulation characterization

identified_antennas: Rectangular Microstrip Antenna (generic background reference in Fig. 1)
Final Patch Obtained after Simulation (Fig. 3; canonical dominant design)
proposed_final_antenna_rationale: The canonical antenna is the 'Final Patch Obtained after Simulation' because the paper explicitly states this as the simulation outcome and pairs it with the measured/simulated performance discussion (return loss, VSWR, impedance, radiation pattern). Fig. 2 is the proposed geometry, but Fig. 3 is the finalized version and Table I supplies the finalized numerical parameters. The generic Fig. 1 illustration is only background and not a separate design. The final simulated design is therefore selected as the dominant proposed/final antenna.
selected_design_rationale: The paper presents one main antenna design rather than competing variants. Fig. 2 introduces the proposed rectangular microstrip patch antenna, and Fig. 3 explicitly labels the 'final patch obtained after simulation,' making it the most authoritative final geometry signal. Table I then gives the finalized dimensions and feed/ground values, so it is the best source for the canonical dimensions. No separate measured/fabricated geometry or alternate design family is provided, so the simulated final patch is treated as the dominant canonical design.
selected_design_summary: Dominant design is a single rectangular microstrip patch antenna with coaxial probe feeding on an FR-4 substrate, intended for the S-band around 2.25 GHz. The canonical final design uses the finalized simulated geometry from Fig. 3 and the parameter set in Table I: rectangular patch, finite/partial ground plane, FR-4 substrate (h = 1.6 mm), and a coaxial feed positioned near (11.66 mm, 20.29 mm).

unresolved_conflicts.description: The prose around Fig. 2 gives patch size 40.1 mm × 31 mm and feed location 11.662 mm by 20.286 mm, while Table I lists Length 31.43 mm, Width 40.57 mm, Xf 11.66 mm, and Yf 20.29 mm. The dedicated parameter table appears more authoritative for the finalized design, but the discrepancy is not explicitly explained.
unresolved_conflicts.topic: patch and feed coordinates: prose vs parameter table

unresolved_conflicts.description: The abstract/conclusion state the antenna is designed to operate over 2 GHz to 2.5 GHz, while one contextual sentence mentions an expected span of 2 GHz to 2.25 GHz. The broader 2 GHz to 2.5 GHz target is treated as the main design intent, but the narrower span remains visible in the retrieved text.
